message: Context-pack output paths are built from the raw task id, so a valid task id containing path separators or '..' can write the pack outside execute/context/<task_id>.md.
Path context selectors are not constrained to repo-relative paths before reading, so a selector like ../outside.txt can resolve evidence from outside the working tree.
execute.loop.settle rejects the fixed default value of 1 even though the contract says only non-default settle values should be rejected.

Context path selectors are joined directly with the repo root, so a selector such as `../outside.txt` can read and include files outside the workspace instead of being treated as an invalid repo-relative selector.
The per-task validation runner invents a new subprocess runner instead of reusing the existing gate validation command runner.
The new plan context command/orchestration path is not covered by tests.
The validation runner timeout path is untested.
The binary/unreadable context selector fallback required by the contract is not tested.

Per-task validation uses a newly hand-rolled command runner instead of reusing the existing gate validation runner, leaving two implementations of the same shell/cwd/env/timeout/process-group/exit-code behavior.
PlanContext runs each task's validation commands twice in sequence on the same working tree.
The workflow documentation does not mention the new user-facing `pactum plan context` command or its generated context-pack artifacts.
